name: Namangan
description: Namangan in northern Fergana is Uzbekistan’s second city after Tashkent by population — industrial and crafts centre, “City of Flowers” with an international flower festival since 1961. Population 700,000+; rich architectural heritage.

monuments.title: Aksikent archaeological site
monuments.info: Ancient city ruins (3rd c. BC–17th c. AD) in Turakurgan on the Syr Darya — major Fergana capital and crafts centre, birthplace of Zahiruddin Muhammad Babur; open-air study site.

monuments.title: Mulla Bozor Okhund complex
monuments.info: 17th c. shrine to scholar Khwaja Ubaydullah Boburkhon, teacher of poet Mashrab; blue dome and courtyard; restored 1990s.

monuments.title: Khoja Amin Mausoleum
monuments.info: 18th c. over Imam Khoja Eshon of the Sheikhantaur line; 9×12 m brick iwan–dome with girih carving.

monuments.title: Valley of Legends (Bobur Park)
monuments.info: Former Bobur Park — one of Central Asia’s largest modern leisure parks (100+ ha): water park, rides, malls and flower festival venues.
